# Video Banner

The Video Banner is a specialized banner for displaying video content as the primary page element. It supports two width configurations — max-width constrained or fully fluid — making it adaptable to different layout needs. Unlike the Grand Hero and Image Banners, it does not include a text content overlay.

## Interactive Preview

Use the controls panel below to experiment with the component's properties in real-time.

## Technical Specifications

- **Atomic Level**: Molecule
- **Template**: `video/yds-video-banner.twig`
- **Styles**: `video/_yds-video-banner.scss`
- **Note**: The Video Banner does not support component theme dials.

### Accessibility

- Video content should include captions for users who are deaf or hard of hearing
- Avoid autoplaying video with audio — this creates accessibility and user experience issues
- Provide a text alternative or transcript when the video conveys critical information
- Ensure video player controls are keyboard accessible

### Behavior

- **Responsive Design**: Video scales proportionally across viewport sizes
- **Width**: `max` constrains the video to the standard content max-width; `full` allows the video to span the full viewport

## Usage Guidelines

### Best Practices

- Use when video is the primary content and no accompanying text or CTA is needed
- Choose `max` width for standard content pages
- Choose `full` width for immersive, cinematic presentations
- Ensure the video embed includes appropriate controls for play, pause, and volume

### Things to Avoid

- Don't autoplay video with audio — it disrupts users and creates accessibility issues
- Don't use when you need text content alongside the video — consider Grand Hero Banner with video enabled instead
- Avoid using multiple video banners on a single page

## Testing

For visual regression testing with both width variations, see the [Visreg page](?path=/story/molecules-banners-video-banner-visreg--visreg).

## Related Components

- **Grand Hero Banner**: For a video background with text content overlay
- **Image Banner**: For a static image in place of video
- **Action Banner**: For hero content with prominent calls-to-action
